steps.title: Identify the given information and set up the proportion
steps.content: The provider surveyed a sample of 300 customers from a total population of 30,000 customers. In the sample, 8 customers said they would be interested in the new plan. To estimate the total number interested in the full population, we use the sample proportion to scale up.

steps.title: Scale the sample to estimate the population
steps.content:
Find the scaling factor from sample to population:
$$\frac{30,000}{300} = 100$$
The population is 100 times the sample size, so multiply the number of interested customers in the sample by 100:
$$8 \times 100 = 800$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
